statement: What organizers of events should not require upon entry is a vaccination certificate.
What the Netherlands should not spend on defense is more money.
What should not become free for all parents at least three days a week is childcare.
What the Netherlands should not leave is the European Union (EU).
What there should not be instead of the tax on car ownership is a tax per kilometer driven for motorists.
What it should not be allowed to set off again during the upcoming New Year's Eve are decorative fireworks.
What there should be is a no additional tax on purchasing meat.
What there should not be is less money for public broadcasting.
What there should not be instead of the existing health insurance companies is a national healthcare fund for everyone.
What the government should not abolish is the ban on face-covering clothing.
What the national government, rather than provinces and municipalities, should not decide is where new residential areas are built.
What the government should not reduce to 5 percent is the VAT on cultural activities.
What the Netherlands should not build is a new nuclear power plant.
Where no housing should be built is on land currently used for agriculture.
What households with two partners, one of whom works, should not receive are the same tax benefits as households with two working partners.
What the Dutch government should not apologize for is the historical slave trade.
What citizens should not be allowed to stop is the opportunity to block laws passed by parliament through a referendum.
What primary school teachers should not start earning is as much as secondary school teachers.
What there should not be are fewer options for community service sentences instead of prison sentences.
What the Netherlands should not introduce for short-distance flights is an additional flight tax.
What asylum seekers with a temporary residence permit should not have to before getting a rental home is integrate.
What should not be legalized are both the purchase and sale of soft drugs by coffee shops.
What the government should no longer make compulsory at universities and colleges is Dutch-language education.
What people who consider their lives complete should not be able to get is help with suicide.
What an increase in minimum wages should still automatically increase is an increase in welfare benefits.
What New residential areas should not consist of is at least 40 percent social housing.
What there should be are new restrictions on farm activities.
What should not be established so that students make a choice between vocational education, general secondary education, or pre-university education at a later age is a middle school.
What the Netherlands should not accept are more refugees than it currently does.
What people should not always be able to choose is whether to wear a face mask.
What limiting rights and freedoms is not necessary to combat is organized crime.
What the growth of Islam is not a threat to is Spain's security.
What current gender policies are not against is men.
What does not improve when they are privatized is the efficiency of public services.
What it should not be easier for companies to fire are workers.
What the state should not take are measures to redistribute wealth from the rich to the poor.
What the government should not increase, even if this means increasing taxes, is spending on public health care.
What is not necessary is a permanent tax on large fortunes and assets.
What should not be reduced without reducing workers' wages is the working day.
What governments should not intervene as little as possible in is the economy.
What taxes on fossil fuels should not be raised to finance is the Green Transition.
What climate change is not solely attributable to is human action.
What the future Spanish government should not increase by means of large water transfers is irrigated agricultural areas.
What we should not recover to better defend Spain's interests in Europe is more sovereignty.
What the Spanish government should not promote is the strengthening of NATO in Europe.
What the best way to solve the conflict in Catalonia is is that its citizens cannot vote on their future in a referendum.
What negotiating with the independentistas does not weaken is the State.
What there is no need to deepen is Spain's territorial decentralization.
What should not be recognized by the Constitution is the right to self-determination.
What the policies of linguistic immersion in the native language of bilingual Autonomous Communities do not endanger is Spanish.
What is not necessary to repeal is the Law of Democratic Memory passed during this legislature.
What Spain should not be more tolerant of is illegal immigration.
What immigrants should not pay for are their own health services.
What housing prices should not be regulated to guarantee is access for all people.
What no general speed limit should apply is on all highways.
What Germany should not increase is its defense spending.
What young people over the age of 16 should not be allowed to vote in are Bundestag elections.
What should not be ended are subsidies for wind energy.
What should not be more strictly limited by law is the ability of landlords to increase housing rents.
What should not continue to be protected by patents are Covid-19 vaccines.
What should not be brought forward is the phase-out of coal-fired power generation planned for 2038.
What all employed persons should not necessarily have to be insured under is the statutory pension scheme.
What should not be abolished is the right of recognized refugees to join their families.
What a national tax should not be levied on is revenue generated in Germany from digital services.
What should not be promoted more strongly than other living arrangements is the traditional family of father, mother and children.
What should not be permitted are donations from companies to political parties.
What Students should receive depending on their parents' income is BAföG.
What it should generally not be possible to have in Germany is a second citizenship in addition to the German one.
What Federal authorities should not use in their publications is different gender identities.
What should not be allowed to go into operation as planned is the Nord Stream 2 Baltic Sea pipeline, which transports gas from Russia to Germany.
What is not to be completely abolished is the solidarity surcharge.
What female civil servants should not be allowed to wear while on duty are headscarves.
What should no longer be possible in the long term is the registration of new cars with combustion engines.
What the federal government should not be given is more responsibilities in school policy.
What the federal government should not provide is more financial support for projects to combat anti-Semitism.
What Chinese companies should be allowed to receive are contracts for the expansion of the communications infrastructure in Germany.
What the state should not collect for religious communities is church tax.
What should not be permitted is the controlled sale of cannabis.
What Germany should not leave is the European Union.
What should not have to be filled alternately by women and men are the state lists of the parties for the elections to the German Bundestag.
What should not be charged at a flat rate per case is inpatient treatment in hospitals.
What a tax should not be levied on is high assets.
What facial recognition software should not be used for is video surveillance in public places.
What married couples without children should not continue to receive are tax breaks.
What should not be promoted more than conventional farming is organic agriculture.
What should not be able to be recognized by the state as religious communities are Islamic associations.
What should not increase more than planned is the government-set price for CO2 emissions from heating and driving.
What should not be retained is the debt brake in the Basic Law.
What asylum should not only be granted to are politically persecuted persons.
What should not be increased to at least 12 euros by 2022 at the latest is the statutory minimum wage.
What should not be taxed more heavily is air traffic.
What companies should not decide for themselves is whether to allow their employees to work from home.
What the European Union should not have less influence on is Polish domestic policy.
What social transfers should not be increased to limit are the effects of inflation on citizens.
What the state should not finance if the waiting time at a public facility exceeds three months are private visits to specialists.
What the state should not provide for every child is a free nursery place.
What schools should not have more freedom to choose is the content covered in the curriculum.
What the state should not build for rent is low-rent apartments.
Who taxes should not be increased for are top earners.
What should not be introduced for those who have worked a certain number of years, regardless of their age, is early retirement.
What should not be liberalized are abortion laws.
What Not all entrepreneurs should pay regardless of income is the same amount of health premium.
What should be binding depending on turnout are the results of certain nationwide referendums.
What Poland should not adopt is the migrant relocation solutions adopted by the European Union.
What should not be strengthened is the independence of the judiciary from parliament and the government.
What should not be further increased is the share of defense spending in Poland's GDP.
What should not be increased at the expense of the central government is the powers of local governments.
What Poland should not move away from no later than 2040 is coal mining.
What Poland should not have blocked is grain imports from Ukraine.
What Christian values should not be the basis of is state social policy.
What should not be restricted is public media funding from the state budget.
What should not be restricted are the powers of the secret services to track the activities of citizens on the Internet.
What the EU's rule of law mechanism does not threaten is Hungary's sovereignty.
What Hungarian foreign policy should not be driven solely by is Hungarian economic interests.
What Hungary should not decide by referendum is whether to remain part of the EU.
What Gender identity should not be influenced by is environmental influences (e.g. media content, sensitising activities)
What is not needed is stronger state regulation of the work of NGOs supported by foreign organisations.
What Hungary should not join is the European Public Prosecutor's Office.
What is not needed is stricter regulation of interception software (e.g. Pegasus) (e.g. subject to judicial authorisation).
What disclosure of the origin of criminals is not necessary for is more effective law enforcement.
What the age of compulsory schooling should not be raised back to is 18.
What education spending should not be increased to is at least the OECD average of 5.2 per cent (GDP).
What should not be doubled are teachers' salaries.
What has not been reduced by changing the university model (reorganisation into a trust) is political influence.
Who should not be allowed to marry are only men and women.
What Parties should not seek to have when drawing up lists is a closer ratio of men to women.
What the state should not take to promote equal participation of fathers and mothers in child-rearing are targeted measures.
What the Hungarian government should not ratify is the Istanbul Convention, which combats violence against women and domestic violence.
What is not an effective way of reducing rationing is the conclusion of favourable gas supply contracts with Russia.
What is not needed is comprehensive public procurement reform (e.g. opening up large-scale centralised public procurement to smaller firms).
What public works do not help people re-enter is the labour market.
What a price freeze on some basic foodstuffs (e.g. chicken tail, milk) is not the right step to fight is inflation.
What is needed is public regulation of the rental housing market.
What should not be extended is the current three-month unemployment benefit.
What is no better way of supporting families than increasing the family allowance is a family tax allowance.
What it is not right that the Education Office, rather than parents and kindergarten teachers, decides on is the school entry maturity of children over the age of six.
Where the use of medical cannabis should not be legalised is in Hungary.
What should not be established is an independent Ministry of Health.
What should not be directly elected is the President of the Hungarian Republic.
What is not needed is comprehensive reform of the electoral system (redrawing of district boundaries, abolition of winner compensation, extension of postal voting).
What there is no need to provide is a legal framework for primary elections.
What should not be 16 or older is voting age for elections.
What should not be free for all is internet access.
What should not be taxed more heavily are polluting companies.
What should not be limited in larger cities through various measures (P+R parking, construction of cycle paths, improvement of public transport) is car traffic.
What the redevelopment of urban green spaces (e.g. the Liget project in Budapest) does not need is a broad social dialogue.
What is not needed is an independent ministry for the environment.
What should not be introduced is an animal rights commissioner.
What European integration is is not an all-positive process.
What Italy should not leave is the Eurozone.
What the European Union should not have is a common foreign policy.
What there should not be is a common European army.
What has not gone too far is European economic integration: what member states should not regain is more autonomy.
What restrictions on personal freedom and privacy is not acceptable to deal with are health emergencies such as Covid-19.
What must not be stopped, even by extreme means, are Migrant landings.
What children born in Italy to foreign citizens and who have completed schooling should not be granted is Italian citizenship (ius scholae).
What more civil rights should not be granted to is homosexual, bisexual, transgender (LGBT+) people.
What citizens should not be guaranteed is freedom of choice in end-of-life matters (euthanasia).
What should not be allowed is recreational use of marijuana/cannabis.
What a law is not needed to prevent from relocating their production abroad are companies.
What should not be introduced is a wealth tax on great wealth.
What businesses should not be able to fire more easily are employees.
What should not only be managed by the state, but also by private individuals is health care.
What the introduction of a single income tax rate ("flat tax") would not benefit is the Italian economy.
What should not be introduced is an hourly minimum wage.
What is not a measure to be cancelled is the citizenship allowance.
What should not be time-limited is beach concessions to private individuals.
What should not be promoted is the use of nuclear power plants for the purpose of producing energy.
What is not a priority for Italy is the construction of Major Works.
What Italy should not build are more incinerators/thermal power plants.
What drilling is not necessary to find are more energy resources.
What are not necessary infrastructure for Italy are Regasifiers.
What Italy should not keep aligned with the choices of the Atlantic Alliance (NATO) is its foreign policy.
What should not be tougher is sanctions against Russia.
What Italy should not stop sending to the Ukrainian government are arms and war material.
Where taxes collected at the regional level should not be fully retained is in the region itself.
What should not be introduced is separation of careers between judges and prosecutors.
What should not be introduced is direct election of the President of the Republic.
What there should not be an increase in is the retirement age.
What the federal government should not allocate for health insurance premium subsidies is more funding.
What the pension is currently limited to for married couples is 150% of the maximum individual AHV pension (capping). What should not be eliminated is this limit.
What are not to be reduced as part of the reform of the BVG (occupational pension plan) are pensions (lowering the minimum conversion rate from 6.8% to 6%).
What paid parental leave should not be increased beyond are today's 14 weeks of maternity leave and two weeks of paternity leave.
What the federal government should not provide for public housing construction is more financial support.
What should not be introduced based on the Swiss vaccination plan is compulsory vaccination of children.
What there should not be an introduction of a tax on is foods containing sugar (sugar tax).
What insured persons should not contribute more to is health care costs (e.g., increase the minimum deductible).
What should not be more limited is the Federal Council's ability to restrict private and economic life in the event of a pandemic.
What the federal government should not be given the authority to determine is the hospital offering (national hospital planning with regard to locations and range of services).
What should not be taught in regular classes according to the Swiss integrated schooling concept are children with learning difficulties or disabilities.
What the federal government should not raise are the requirements for the high school.
What the state should not be more committed to are equal educational opportunities (e.g., through subsidized remedial courses for students from low-income families).
What should not be relaxed are the conditions for naturalization (e.g., shorter residence period).
What should not be allowed to work in Switzerland are more qualified workers from non-EU/EFTA countries (increase third-country quota).
What there should not be are efforts to house asylum seekers in centers outside Europe during the asylum procedure.
What foreign nationals who have lived in Switzerland for at least ten years should not be granted is the right to vote and stand for election at the municipal level.
What should not be legalized is cannabis use.
What doctors should not be allowed to administer is direct active euthanasia.
What should not be introduced alongside "female" and "male" is a third official gender.
What Same-sex couples should not have is the same rights as heterosexual couples in all areas.
What there should not be at the federal level over the next four years is tax cuts.
What are not to be taxed separately are married couples (individual taxation).
What there should not be the introduction of a national inheritance tax on are all inheritances over one million Swiss francs.
What should not be further reduced through fiscal equalization are the differences between cantons with high and low financial capacity.
What should not be introduced is a minimum wage of CHF 4,000 for all full-time employees.
What there should not be for the financial sector are stricter regulations (e.g., stricter capital requirements for banks, ban on bonuses).
What private households should not be free to choose is their electricity supplier (complete liberalization of the electricity market).
What should not be relaxed are housing construction regulations (e.g., noise protection, occupancy rates).
What there should not be are stricter controls on equal pay for women and men.
What should not be widened are busy sections of highways.
What Switzerland should not ban starting in 2035 is the registration of new passenger cars with internal combustion engines.
What should not be relied on exclusively, rather than bans and restrictions, to achieve climate targets, are incentives and target agreements.
What it's fair that environmental and landscape protection rules are not being relaxed to allow for is the development of renewable energy.
What should not be allowed again is the construction of new nuclear power plants.
What the state should not guarantee is a comprehensive public service offering also in rural regions.
What should not be introduced is increasing electricity tariffs when consumption is higher (progressive electricity tariffs).
What should not be relaxed are the protection regulations for large predators (lynx, wolf, bear).
What direct payments should not only be granted to are farmers with proof of ecological performance.
What there should not be for livestock are stricter animal welfare regulations (e.g. permanent access to outdoor areas).
What 30% of Switzerland's land area should not be dedicated to is preserving biodiversity.
What there should not be is a ban on single-use plastic and non-recyclable plastics.

What there should not be are government measures to make the use of electronic devices more sustainable (e.g., right to repair, extension of warranty period, minimum guaranteed period for software updates).
What the Swiss mobile network should not be equipped throughout the country with is the latest technology (currently 5G standard).
What the federal government should not be given in order to be able to impose binding directives and standards on the cantons is additional powers in the area of digitization of government services.
What there should not be is a stronger regulation of the major Internet platforms (i.e., transparency rules on algorithms, increased liability for content, combating disinformation).
What should not be is a popular initiative aims to reduce television and radio fees (CHF 200 per household, exemption for businesses).
What the voting age should not be lowered to is 16.
What it should not be possible to hold is a referendum on federal spending above a certain amount (optional financial referendum).
What the army's target number of soldiers should not expand to is at least 120,000.
What the Swiss Armed Forces should not expand is their cooperation with NATO.

What the Federal Council should not be allowed to authorize to re-export Swiss weapons in cases of a war of aggression in violation of international law (e.g., the attack on Ukraine) are other states.
What should not be banned in public spaces is automatic facial recognition.
What Switzerland should not terminate is the Schengen agreement with the EU and what Switzerland should not reintroduce directly on the border are more security checks.
What there should not be closer relations with is the European Union (EU).
What Switzerland should not strive for is a comprehensive free trade agreement (including agriculture) with the USA.
What companies should not be obliged to ensure that their subsidiaries and suppliers operating abroad comply with are social and environmental standards.
What Switzerland should not terminate are the Bilateral Agreements with the EU and what Switzerland should not seek is a free trade agreement without the free movement of persons.
What Switzerland should not return to is a strict interpretation of neutrality (renounce economic sanctions to a large extent).
